articles.title: The manager who knows how to upset Chelsea in the FA Cup
articles.summary: Phil Parkinson is the most recent manager of a lower division side to knock Chelsea out of the FA Cup and is aiming to create more history this weekend.

articles.title: 'Slot needs something to keep him in a job' - could the FA Cup be it?
articles.summary: Despite a torrid Premier League campaign, could winning the FA Cup keep Liverpool manager Arne Slot in a job?

articles.title: Who is to blame for Spurs' chaotic descent towards relegation?
articles.summary: BBC Sport's chief football writer Phil McNulty examines who - or what - is to blame for Spurs' crisis as they fight to avoid relegation.

articles.title: 'What is a foul? What is a contact?' - Nuno calls for clarity
articles.summary: West Ham manager Nuno Espirito Santo is calling for clarity on what constitutes a foul at set-pieces, warning current levels of grappling and blocking are "almost crazy".

articles.title: Challenges are Celtic 'wake-up call' - O'Neill
articles.summary: The possibility of Celtic ending the season without a trophy "should be a wake-up call for the football club", interim manager Martin O'Neill says.

articles.title: 'Howard Webb, where are you?' - How we can stop VAR ruining football
articles.summary: In his BBC Sport column, former Premier League boss Tony Pulis explains how VAR has become a monster, and why people in the game should be asked how to fix it.

articles.title: Early goals send Falkirk to Scottish Cup semi-finals
articles.summary: Two early goals help Falkirk make it by Dundee United to reach the Scottish Cup semi-finals.

articles.title: Bayern hammer Monchengladbach to move 14 points clear
articles.summary: Bayern Munich win their fifth match in a row with a 4-1 win over Borussia Monchengladbach, to move to 14 points clear at the top of the Bundesliga table.

articles.title: 'As pure as you can hit it!' Robertson fires Liverpool into lead
articles.summary: Liverpool left-back Andrew Robertson scores a superb opening goal for his side in their FA Cup fifth round tie against Wolves at Molineux.

articles.title: 'We don't know what is a foul' - Nuno wants set-piece review
articles.summary: With increased attention on grappling at corner kicks in the Premier League, West Ham head coach Nuno Espirito Santo wants officials to explain what is and is not a foul.

articles.title: Rangers 'have to' lift silverware, says Rohl ahead of Celtic game
articles.summary: Rangers manager Danny Rohl says Sunday's game with Celtic is a "great opportunity to make a good step towards" winning a trophy.

articles.title: Sanchez 'disappointed' but Rosenior doesn't have a number one goalkeeper
articles.summary: Chelsea head coach Liam Rosenior says goalkeeper Robert Sanchez was "really disappointed" to be left out at Aston Villa.

articles.title: From Rochdale to Premier League: the making of Mane
articles.summary: Teenage forward Mateus Mane has been a rare bright light in Wolves' season as BBC Sport charts his journey from Portugal to the Premier League.

articles.title: Pick your England XI to face Iceland
articles.summary: Choose who England manager Sarina Wiegman should name in her line-up to play Iceland on Saturday.

articles.title: Footballer pleads not guilty to assaulting woman
articles.summary: Former Premier League winger Jordan Ibe is bailed to appear for trial in July.

articles.title: Leeds condemn boos and urge respect for Ramadan break
articles.summary: Leeds United call on supporters to show respect when players who are observing Ramadan break their fast during Sunday's FA Cup match against Norwich City.

articles.title: 'Stick with the plan' - Patience key for England, says Wiegman
articles.summary: England manager Sarina Wiegman says it is "very important" her team are patient if goals do not come quickly in their 2027 Women's World Cup qualifier against Iceland on Saturday.
